claims.title: Bootstrap, discovery, and startup reporting
claims.summary: AddKoan composes referenced capabilities and reports the resulting runtime decisions.

claims.title: Entity<T> data semantics and context
claims.summary: Entities expose provider-bounded persistence, query, paging, streaming, and ambient context semantics.

claims.title: Local JSON and InMemory Entity persistence
claims.summary: JSON supplies Koan's inspectable automatic local floor while InMemory supplies an explicitly ephemeral host-scoped provider and conformance oracle.

claims.title: Durable local SQLite Entity persistence
claims.summary: SQLite supplies the durable local application path over the shared relational contract, including provider-correct filtering, paging, schema ownership, readiness, and package-first template use.

claims.title: PostgreSQL Entity persistence
claims.summary: PostgreSQL preserves Koan Entity CRUD, query, paging, provider-bounded streaming, source routing, schema policy, health, and declared isolation semantics through an application-facing connector and a module-free shared Npgsql mechanism.

claims.title: SQL Server Entity persistence
claims.summary: SQL Server preserves Koan Entity CRUD, native filtering, paging, provider-bounded streaming, batch and conditional writes, source routing, schema policy, health, and declared isolation semantics over the supported relational foundation.

claims.title: Inert layered-provider connection intent
claims.summary: The module-free Zen Garden contract assembly supplies shared connection-intent, offering identity, catalog, subscription, and capability-wish vocabulary without registering services, opening connections, or activating a runtime.

claims.title: MongoDB Entity persistence
claims.summary: MongoDB preserves Koan Entity CRUD, native filtering, explicit paging, provider-bounded streaming, batch and conditional writes, source-aware pooling and routing, readiness, and declared isolation semantics while optional layered connection intent remains inert unless explicitly activated.

claims.title: Couchbase Entity persistence
claims.summary: Couchbase preserves Koan Entity CRUD, native filtering, explicit paging, provider-bounded streaming, conditional and transactional writes, source-aware pooling and routing, participation-owned readiness, and declared isolation semantics within its stated operational limits.

claims.title: Shared Redis backend lifecycle
claims.summary: The functional Redis backend owns discovery, endpoint resolution, host-lifetime connection pooling, and corrective connection failures while its module-author contract remains inert when referenced alone.

claims.title: Redis Entity persistence
claims.summary: Redis supplies keyed, TTL-aware Entity persistence, source and logical-database routing, fast keyed removal, caller-bounded result pages, participation-owned readiness, and explicit rejection of provider-bounded streaming within its stated scan-cost limits.

claims.title: CockroachDB Entity persistence
claims.summary: CockroachDB preserves Koan Entity CRUD, native filtering, paging, provider-bounded streaming, source routing, additive schema policy, participation-owned readiness, and declared isolation semantics through a thin pg-wire delta over the supported relational foundation.

claims.title: Backend discovery and negotiation
claims.summary: Referenced adapters participate in capability-aware selection with inspectable decisions and corrective rejection.

claims.title: Web/API conventions
claims.summary: Entity controllers expose business-readable HTTP APIs through framework conventions.

claims.title: Entity Events and Transport
claims.summary: Entities and entity sequences express local-first Events and Transport intent through the automatic in-process provider with bounded, inspectable delivery semantics.

claims.title: RabbitMQ Communication carriage
claims.summary: A direct RabbitMQ connector reference elects authenticated cross-process Transport and framework carriage without silent local fallback; heterogeneous schema evolution remains outside the guarantee.

claims.title: Jobs and scheduling
claims.summary: Entities own inspectable background work with retries, cancellation, schedules, context restoration, and capability-graded durability.

claims.title: Cache and distributed state
claims.summary: Typed cache semantics work across local and external engines within their stated topology limits.

claims.title: Media processing and serving
claims.summary: Media recipes describe transformation and HTTP-serving behavior over storage-backed assets.

claims.title: AI runtime semantics
claims.summary: The inert AI contracts and compiled runtime expose one Client facade, activate referenced providers once with DI-owned lifetime, and route only through sources that advertise the requested capability while preserving corrective failures.

claims.title: Provider-neutral Entity vector semantics
claims.summary: Vector<TEntity> supplies exact provider election, source and segmentation folds, bounded similarity queries, capability-gated operations, and participation reporting without leaking adapter construction into application code.

claims.title: Local AI provider composition
claims.summary: Ollama, LM Studio, and in-process ONNX providers join one compiled AddKoan topology with explicit-intent precedence, provider-owned discovery mechanics, DI-owned lifetime, and source routing within their stated runtime and model boundaries.

claims.title: AI prompt semantics and HTTP projection
claims.summary: Inspectable prompt values remain inert until used, optional Entity-backed prompt versions resolve deliberately, and a Web package reference projects the compiled AI runtime without registration ceremony.

claims.title: Elasticsearch and OpenSearch vector providers
claims.summary: Elasticsearch and OpenSearch execute Koan vector semantics through one shared runtime while preserving native kNN dialects, selected-provider naming, and participation-owned readiness within stated limits.

claims.title: Local in-memory and sqlite-vec vector providers
claims.summary: In-memory supplies Koan's automatic ephemeral semantic floor while sqlite-vec preserves the same Entity intent with embedded durability, SQLite placement pairing, selected-provider naming, and participation-owned readiness within stated limits.

claims.title: Qdrant, Milvus, and Weaviate vector providers
claims.summary: Qdrant, Milvus, and Weaviate preserve Koan's Entity vector intent while owning their native schema, filter, consistency, discovery, and wire guarantees; query cardinality, selected-provider naming, and participation readiness remain framework-owned.

claims.title: MCP and agent-facing surfaces
claims.summary: Koan exposes application tools and self-description to coding and runtime agents through MCP.

claims.title: Optional Entity HTTP, OpenAPI, and SSE projections
claims.summary: Referenced Web add-ons compose through AddKoan(): richer Entity controllers remain host-owned, OpenAPI resolves one document/UI policy, and one SSE result model serves controllers and framework transports.

claims.title: Entity social-card projection
claims.summary: A host-owned Entity declaration projects encoded OpenGraph, Twitter, title, and canonical metadata into SPA navigations without manual middleware or SSR.

claims.title: Local-first tenant isolation and administration
claims.summary: Referencing Tenancy contributes one hard ambient segmentation guarantee across active pillars; the optional Web projection mounts host-authorized tenant-registry, deterministic membership, and administration-audit surfaces without lifecycle-shaped placeholders.

claims.title: Entity field-at-rest classification
claims.summary: Writable classified string Entity properties are authenticated-encrypted through Data-owned transforms; active segmentation scopes key custody automatically, Development is zero-config, and non-Development hosts require an application key provider.

claims.title: Authentication, authorization, and client token issuance
claims.summary: Durable Identity, web sign-in, authenticated identity management, active-membership tenant scoping, and one ES256 workload-token verifier compose through referenced modules; the embedded OAuth server adds audience-bound public-client issuance and durable key continuity, while external provider behavior remains connector-specific.

claims.title: Testing and local infrastructure
claims.summary: Application, framework, and provider authors inherit Entity conformance, boot the real compiled Koan host, and reuse fail-closed container-backed infrastructure with owned ambient and async lifecycle.

claims.title: Durable local SQLite Cache
claims.summary: A SQLite Cache reference replaces the memory Local floor with exact-tagged, sliding-expiration-aware state that survives complete host restart without changing Entity cache intent.

claims.title: Recoverable Entity deletion
claims.summary: Selected Entity types replace physical removal with hidden managed deletion while explicit type-scoped recycle-bin, restore, and purge verbs preserve other filters and authorization boundaries.

claims.title: Authenticated Development runtime diagnostics
claims.summary: A Web Admin reference mounts one authenticated, read-only Development projection of canonical Koan provenance, health, environment, and bounded process facts with permanent secret and host-identity redaction.

claims.title: Deterministic local OAuth and OIDC testing
claims.summary: A Development test-provider reference supplies self-hosted OAuth2 and OIDC flows that exercise Web Auth challenge, callback, token validation, claim mapping, and identity linking without production identity credentials.

claims.title: Package-first installation
claims.summary: The foundation bundle, web application bundle, and templates install through ordinary NuGet and dotnet new flows, preserve independent package identity, and reach the documented first business result outside the repository.

claims.title: Preview upgrade and interrupted-release recovery
claims.summary: A later 0.20 package wave must prove that a public-created application upgrades cleanly and that interrupted publication recovers from immutable evidence.

claims.title: Operations, health, and diagnostics
claims.summary: Runtime facts and health expose bounded host decisions; the optional Observability reference composes one inspectable OpenTelemetry pipeline across every Koan trace source and meter.

claims.title: Canonical Entity pipelines
claims.summary: Canon discovers every canonical Entity, compiles one deterministic host-owned pipeline plan, commits canonical state, indexes, and audit in an explicit non-atomic order, and optionally projects the same plan through inspectable HTTP surfaces.
